questions.text: Microorganisms that can reproduce only inside the cells of a host organism are:
questions.explanation: Viruses are borderline between living and non-living; they require a host cell to replicate.

questions.text: The process of conversion of sugar into alcohol is called:
questions.explanation: Louis Pasteur discovered fermentation in 1857.

questions.text: Which of the following is an antibiotic?
questions.explanation: Streptomycin, tetracycline and erythromycin are common antibiotics made from fungi and bacteria.

questions.text: The bread dough rises because of:
questions.explanation: Yeast reproduces rapidly and produces carbon dioxide during respiration, which fills the dough.

questions.text: Carrier of malaria-causing protozoan is:
questions.explanation: Female Anopheles carries the parasite Plasmodium.

questions.text: The most common carrier of communicable diseases is:
questions.explanation: Houseflies sit on garbage and animal excreta, carrying pathogens to uncovered food.

questions.text: Which microorganism contains chlorophyll?
questions.explanation: Algae like Spirogyra and Chlamydomonas are autotrophic and contain chlorophyll.

questions.text: The bacterium that helps in the formation of curd is:
questions.explanation: Lactobacillus promotes the formation of curd from milk by multiplying in it.

questions.text: Who discovered the vaccine for smallpox?
questions.explanation: Edward Jenner discovered the smallpox vaccine in 1798.

questions.text: Antibiotics are effective against:
questions.explanation: Antibiotics do not work against viruses; they target bacterial cell walls or processes.

questions.text: Atmospheric nitrogen is fixed in the soil by ________ to increase fertility.
questions.explanation: Some bacteria and blue green algae can fix nitrogen from the atmosphere.

questions.text: Cholera is caused by:
questions.explanation: Cholera is a bacterial disease spread through contaminated water or food.

questions.text: Which of these is a fungal disease in plants?
questions.explanation: Rust of wheat is caused by fungi and is air-borne.

questions.text: Amoeba belongs to the group of:
questions.explanation: Protozoa are single-celled microscopic animals like Amoeba and Paramecium.

questions.text: Sodium benzoate and sodium metabisulphite are common:
questions.explanation: These are chemical preservatives used to check the growth of microorganisms in jams and squashes.

questions.text: Mushroom is a type of:
questions.explanation: Mushrooms are multicellular fungi that feed on decaying matter.

questions.text: Who discovered the first antibiotic, Penicillin?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming discovered Penicillin in 1929 from a mold called Penicillium notatum.

questions.text: The science of study of microorganisms is called:
questions.explanation: Microbiology deals with organisms that are too small to be seen with the naked eye.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in the production of vinegar?
questions.explanation: Yeast is used for commercial production of alcohol and wine, which is then converted to acetic acid (vinegar).

questions.text: Tuberculosis is caused by:
questions.explanation: TB is a bacterial disease primarily affecting the lungs.

questions.text: Preservation of milk by heating it to 70°C and then suddenly chilling it is:
questions.explanation: Pasteurization kills harmful microbes without changing the taste of milk.

questions.text: Foot and mouth disease of cattle is caused by a:
questions.explanation: It is a highly contagious viral disease of clover-hoofed animals.

questions.text: Rhizobium bacteria are found in the root nodules of ________ plants.
questions.explanation: Leguminous plants like peas and beans have a symbiotic relationship with Rhizobium.

questions.text: Which microorganism is used to produce large scale alcohol?
questions.explanation: Yeast ferments sugars in grains like barley and wheat to produce alcohol.

questions.text: Dengue is spread by:
questions.explanation: The female Aedes mosquito acts as a carrier for the Dengue virus.

questions.text: Microorganisms that cause diseases are called:
questions.explanation: Pathogens are disease-causing microbes that enter our body through air, water, or food.

questions.text: Yeast is used in making idlis and dhosals to provide:
questions.explanation: The CO2 bubbles produced by yeast/fermentation create pores and make the food soft.

questions.text: Anthrax is a dangerous human and cattle disease caused by a:
questions.explanation: Robert Koch discovered the bacterium (Bacillus anthracis) which causes anthrax.

questions.text: Common cold and Influenza (Flu) are caused by:
questions.explanation: Most common respiratory infections are viral in nature.

questions.text: The percentage of nitrogen in the atmosphere is approximately:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is the most abundant gas in our atmosphere.

questions.text: Salt and edible oils are used to check microbial growth. They are called:
questions.explanation: Salting draws out water from cells, preventing microbial growth.

questions.text: Which of these cannot be seen even with a powerful light microscope?
questions.explanation: Viruses are so small they can only be seen with an electron microscope.

questions.text: Malaria is a ________ disease.
questions.explanation: It is caused by the Plasmodium protozoan.

questions.text: Polio and Chicken Pox are caused by:
questions.explanation: These are common viral diseases for which vaccines are available.

questions.text: Algae mostly live in:
questions.explanation: Most algae are aquatic, found in both fresh and marine water.

questions.text: Dry fruits and vegetables are sold in sealed air-tight packets to prevent:
questions.explanation: Microbes need air and moisture to grow; air-tight packing removes these factors.

questions.text: A common preservative used in jams and pickles is:
questions.explanation: Sugar is used in jams, while salt and vinegar are common in pickles.

questions.text: Which of these is not a group of microorganisms?
questions.explanation: Insects are animals visible to the naked eye and are not classified as microorganisms.

questions.text: Which bacterium lives in our gut and helps in digestion?
questions.explanation: Certain strains of E. coli are part of the normal flora of the human gut.

questions.text: Vaccination works by producing ________ in our body.
questions.explanation: Antibodies are produced to fight the weakened/dead microbes in the vaccine, creating immunity.
